sentence: Chapter~2 argued that decode performance is a residency problem: data must live in the right tier of the memory hierarchy long enough to amortize movement cost .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: That argument is not hardware-agnostic poetry---every compiler pass in Parts~IV--VI must eventually lower to concrete limits on bandwidth, SRAM capacity, parallelism grain, and dynamic-shape legality .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: This chapter maps those limits across GPUs, CPUs, and NPUs, then states how hardware-aware compilation (tiling, layout, and pass ordering) encodes the map into IR so automated tools such as YiRage can target multiple silicon generations without rewriting the book's engineering logic .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: Industrial inference fleets rarely standardize on a single accelerator class .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: Hyperscale decode pools are still dominated by NVIDIA GPUs with Hopper- and Blackwell-generation tensor cores, asynchronous copy engines, and CUDA Graphs capture for launch reduction .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: AMD Instinct and Ryzen AI lines expose overlapping but non-identical matrix-instruction paths and wavefront scheduling rules; compiler backends must not treat ``GPU'' as a monolith .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: CPUs remain the fallback for small models, heterogeneous orchestration, and pre/post-processing that cannot justify discrete accelerator residency .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: Edge NPUs---including AMD XDNA spatial arrays---trade dynamic flexibility for deterministic on-chip dataflow and watt caps that only static megakernels can satisfy at batch-1 .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: The landscape table is intentionally coarse: later chapters refine NVIDIA memory hierarchy (Chapter~4), XDNA tile rules (Chapter~5), and per-compiler lowering strategies (Part~VI) .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: What matters here is the contract each class offers the decode compiler: GPUs reward fused stages that fit shared memory and tensor-core shapes; CPUs reward cache-resident tiles and NUMA-aware placement; NPUs reward fully scheduled buffer lifetimes with no host-side surprises .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: On-chip SRAM caps how much of a decoder layer can remain resident between sub-operations; exceeding the cap forces spills to HBM or DDR, often erasing fusion wins .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Parallel grain determines whether a schedule is expressed as warps, OpenMP teams, or spatial lanes---mixing grains within one fused stage usually inserts barriers that decode cannot afford .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Dynamic shapes separate server GPUs from edge NPUs: variable sequence lengths and batch sizes are routine in cloud decode, while many NPUs require compile-time specialization of buffer descriptors and DMA chains .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: Use the matrix as a checklist before writing a kernel or a compiler pass: if a proposed fusion increases SRAM footprint on GPU but removes a launch on CPU, the pass must expose both costs in the IR so autotuning can pick per target .

title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: NVIDIA decode optimization is bounded by the interaction of warps, shared memory, and tensor cores .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Warps are the SIMT scheduling unit: branch divergence and barrier placement within a warp directly affect how much useful memory bandwidth is delivered per cycle .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Shared memory is the programmer-visible on-chip scratchpad that makes attention and MLP fusion legal; its capacity (configuration-dependent on Hopper-class parts) is the hard ceiling for MegaKernel stages that refuse to spill to HBM .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Tensor cores accelerate narrow GEMMs and attention inner loops, but only when layouts and instruction shapes match MMA requirements---decode's thin M matrices frequently under-fill tensor cores unless batched or fused with epilogue math .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Hopper-and-later generations add asynchronous copy (TMA) and cluster-scope synchronization, shifting the design question from ``how many kernels'' to ``how many bytes cross each hierarchy level per token'' .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: CUDA Graphs reduce launch tax but do not relax bandwidth floors; they are complementary to fusion, not a substitute .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Compiler passes that target GPUs must therefore reason about warp occupancy, shared-memory allocation, and tensor-core legality together, not as independent knobs .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: CPU decode is often dismissed as slow, yet it remains operationally critical for hybrid serving, development parity, and regions without GPU quota.
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Decoder layers stream large weight matrices through caches too small to retain full parameters; effective decode therefore depends on blocking GEMMs and attention so that hot tiles remain in L2/L3 across tokens .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: SIMD units (AVX-512, AMX, NEON) raise throughput only when layouts are contiguous and epilogues are vectorized---operator-at-a-time frameworks often leave SIMD under-used because each op reloads weights from memory .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: NUMA effects appear when KV cache and weight shards land on remote sockets; a fusion pass that is neutral on a single-socket GPU build can regress on dual-socket Xeon hosts if buffer lifetimes ignore socket affinity .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: Hardware-aware CPU lowering must insert explicit tiling and layout transforms (NCHW vs.\ blocked layouts) and annotate parallel regions with cache-blocking sizes derived from L2/L3 measurements, not datasheet peaks alone .
title: Compiler-aware AI Hardware Design for Edge Devices

sentence: Edge and embedded NPU accelerators optimize for energy per token and deterministic latency under watt caps .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: Many arrays---including AMD AI Engine tiles in XDNA products---implement static dataflow graphs with local SRAM per processing element and restricted dynamic reconfiguration .
title: AMD XDNA Architecture

sentence: Static schedules mean buffer lifetimes, DMA descriptors, and fusion boundaries must be resolved at compile time; runtime op dispatch analogous to PyTorch eager mode is either unsupported or prohibitively expensive .
title: Neural Architecture Search for Automotive Grid Fusion Networks Under Embedded Hardware Constraints

sentence: NPUs therefore amplify the Chapter~2 dataflow mindset: decode either fits as a spatial pipeline with on-chip residency, or it falls off the device to DDR and fails power envelopes .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: Dynamic shapes (variable context length, speculative decoding branches) require explicit recompilation or padded specialization; compiler stacks must surface these legality checks early rather than failing in backend codegen .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: Hardware-aware compilation is the process of lowering the same high-level decoder IR while respecting per-target tiling, layout, and pass ordering constraints .
title: Hardware-Aware Neural Architecture Search (NAS) with Area, Power, and Latency Constraints for ASIC Implementation

sentence: Tiling chooses tile sizes for matmul and attention so that working sets fit GPU shared memory, CPU cache levels, or NPU PE buffers; illegal tiles are rejected before codegen, not discovered in profiling .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: Layout decisions (row-major vs.\ blocked layouts, swizzles for tensor cores, NPU DMA alignment) determine whether fused epilogues vectorize or silently scalarize .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: Pass pipelines order bufferization, fusion, layout normalization, and synchronization insertion differently per target: GPUs may fuse before aggressive layout transforms; NPUs may require lifetime analysis before any algebraic fusion .
title: Hybe: GPU-NPU Hybrid System for Efficient LLM Inference with Million-Token Context Window

sentence: A practical rule used throughout this book: every pass must declare which constraint dimension it optimizes (SRAM footprint, launch count, bandwidth, legality) and which hardware classes it applies to .

sentence: Three recurring anti-patterns destroy cross-hardware compiler credibility: (1)~porting CUDA tile sizes directly to NPU PE arrays without remeasuring on-chip SRAM; (2)~assuming CPU decode can reuse GPU fusion boundaries when weight layouts differ; (3)~benchmarking only ms/token on GPUs while ignoring launches/token and bytes/token on all targets .
title: Characterization of Machine Learning Compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs

sentence: Each anti-pattern is a residency failure expressed as a methodology failure---the fix is always to re-derive the dataflow contract for the target class before tuning instructions .

sentence: YiRage-style chip models should be versioned in the same artifact registry as model weights: a decode graph compiled against Hopper-sm90 must not silently run on sm80 parts with narrower shared memory .

sentence: Independent limit studies show that many decoder configurations are memory-bound across GPU generations even when tensor cores sit idle .
